messages.content:
Create a retrieval-oriented summary for this parent section of a document.
Use the child summaries below. Mention the topics, entities, dates, tables,
figures, definitions, procedures, claims, metrics, and evidence types covered
when they are visible. Do not invent facts. Return only the summary text.

Parent:
{
  "node_id": "0123",
  "title": "媒体声音",
  "page_index": 67,
  "page_range": "67-68"
}

Child summaries:
[
  {
    "node_id": "0124",
    "title": "白鲸出海",
    "page_range": "68-69",
    "summary": "This section can answer retrieval queries regarding:\n- **Topics:** Chinese mobile game overseas expansion trends, genre-specific growth drivers (SLG, Merge casual, Hybrid casual), and market strategies for 2025–2026.\n- **Entities:** Games (*Whiteout Survival*, *Kingshot*, *Last Z*, *Land of Prison*, *Gossip Harbor*); Genres (SLG, Merge/Synthesis casual, Match-3, Hybrid casual); Developer regions (Chinese publishers, Turkish studios).\n- **Dates/Timeframes:** 2025 (current market performance and trends), 2026 (projected industry focus and expansion).\n- **Definitions/Concepts:** SLG (strategy genre), Merge gameplay, Hybrid casual, LTV (Lifetime Value), secondary vs. main gameplay integration, short-drama narrative mechanics.\n- **Procedures/Strategies:** Adapting PC/Steam premium game mechanics for mobile; scaling live-ops event frequency; integrating high-intensity, plot-twist narratives; benchmarking and iterating on Turkish hybrid casual models.\n- **Claims:** SLG is the largest game subcategory in 2025; Merge games achieved >50% YoY market growth and now equal one-third of the Match-3 market size; Chinese developers (including mid/small teams) will successfully penetrate more genres and expand market share in 2026.\n- **Metrics:** New SLG titles exceeding $10M monthly revenue; ~100 monthly in-game events for *Gossip Harbor*; >50% YoY growth for the Merge game market; Merge market size at 1/3 of Match-3.\n- **Evidence Types:** Industry market reports, revenue tracking data, live-ops operational metrics, comparative genre market sizing, trend analysis.\n- **Tables/Figures:** None explicitly referenced or described in the text."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0125",
    "title": "独立出海联合体",
    "page_range": "69-70",
    "summary": "This section reviews the Chinese game export market from 2023 to 2025 and can answer retrieval questions across the following dimensions:\n\n- **Topics:** Chinese game overseas expansion trends, cyclical industry adjustments, domestic vs. overseas mini-game market dynamics, traditional mobile game export consolidation, and PC/console (Steam) market developments.\n- **Entities:** Platforms/Publishers: Tencent, TikTok, Moonton (沐瞳). Notable Titles: 《抓大鹅》, 《永远的蔚蓝海岸》, 《生存33天》, 《三角洲行动》, 《少女前线 2》, 《无尽对决》, 《黑神话：悟空》 (2024), 《明末：渊墟之羽》, 《饿殍：明末千里行》, 《峰火十四》.\n- **Dates:** Late 2020 (start of product model exploration), 2023 (end of exploration cycle/market testing phase), 2024 (release of 《黑神话：悟空》), 2025 (current review period, Tencent policy shift, new title releases).\n- **Definitions/Concepts:** \"Adjustment\" phase (contrasted between 2023's product-model testing and 2025's return to traffic-driven strategies); \"Traffic-driven打法\" (user acquisition/ad-buying focus); \"Numerical-value导向模型\" (stat/progression-driven game design paired with ad spending).\n- **Procedures/Strategies:** Industry pivot from experimental product models to established traffic-acquisition strategies; cross-regional promotion of legacy titles to sustain growth; platform revenue-sharing policy adjustments.\n- **Claims:** The 2023–2025 \"adjustment\" period differs in nature: 2023 tested late-2020 product models, while 2025 reflects a retreat to traffic-driven methods after exploration failures. Domestic mini-games are booming, but overseas lacks equivalent traffic aggregation, delaying TikTok's mini-game platform红利. Traditional mobile exports are consolidating around top publishers; mid-tier (B+ and A-tier) markets are contracting, and numerical-value + ad-buying models are losing effectiveness. Steam/console markets s..."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0126",
    "title": "扬帆出海",
    "page_range": "70-71",
    "summary": "**Topics:** Chinese enterprise overseas expansion trends (2025), gaming vs. non-gaming sector performance, international market layout, genre revenue distribution, and innovation-driven growth strategies.\n**Entities:** Chinese self-developed games, mobile games, United States, Japan, South Korea, Europe, Strategy/SLG genre.\n**Dates/Timeframes:** 2025; six consecutive years (overseas revenue exceeding 100 billion RMB).\n**Metrics:** 2025 self-developed game overseas actual sales revenue: $20.455 billion (+10.23% YoY); mobile game contribution: $18.478 billion (+13.16% YoY); Strategy/SLG revenue share among top 100 overseas self-developed mobile games: 49.96%.\n**Claims:** 2025 marks a \"quality leap\" phase for Chinese overseas expansion; growth is sustained by technological innovation and business model upgrades; US, Japan, and Korea remain core markets while Europe expands steadily; gameplay fusion is a key growth driver; gaming success relies on genre specialization and cultural empowerment, while non-gaming success relies on industrial upgrading and model innovation.\n**Evidence Types:** Industry revenue statistics, year-over-year growth rates, market share percentages, qualitative market trend analysis.\n**Definitions/Procedures/Tables/Figures:** Not addressed in this section."
  }
]

message.content:
**Topics:** Chinese mobile game and enterprise overseas expansion trends (2023–2026), genre-specific growth drivers (SLG, Merge/Hybrid casual), market consolidation vs. innovation, domestic vs. overseas mini-game dynamics, and PC/console (Steam) market developments.
**Entities:** Publishers/Platforms (Tencent, TikTok, Moonton, Chinese publishers, Turkish studios); Games/Titles (*Whiteout Survival*, *Kingshot*, *Last Z*, *Land of Prison*, *Gossip Harbor*, 《抓大鹅》, 《永远的蔚蓝海岸》, 《生存33天》, 《三角洲行动》, 《少女前线 2》, 《无尽对决》, 《黑神话：悟空》, 《明末：渊墟之羽》, 《饿殍：明末千里行》, 《峰火十四》); Markets/Regions (US, Japan, South Korea, Europe, China).
**Dates/Timeframes:** Late 2020 (start of product model exploration), 2023 (market testing/adjustment phase), 2024 (major title releases), 2025 (current review period, "quality leap" phase, strategic pivot), 2026 (projected industry focus), and a six-year consecutive milestone (overseas revenue >100B RMB).
**Definitions/Concepts:** SLG, Merge/Synthesis casual, Hybrid casual, LTV, secondary vs. main gameplay integration, short-drama narrative mechanics, "Adjustment" phase (contrasting 2023 product-model testing with 2025 traffic-driven retreat), "Traffic-driven打法" (user acquisition/ad-buying focus), "Numerical-value导向模型" (stat/progression-driven design paired with ad spending).
**Procedures/Strategies:** Adapting PC/Steam premium mechanics for mobile; scaling live-ops event frequency; integrating high-intensity, plot-twist narratives; benchmarking Turkish hybrid casual models; pivoting from experimental product models to established traffic-acquisition strategies; cross-regional promotion of legacy titles; platform revenue-sharing policy adjustments; leveraging gameplay fusion and cultural empowerment.
**Claims:** SLG is the largest game subcategory in 2025; Merge games achieved >50% YoY growth, now equaling one-third of the Match-3 market size; Chinese developers will successfully penetrate more genres and expand market share in 2026; the 2023–2025 adjustment period reflects a shift from product-model testing to traffic-driven methods after exploration failures; domestic mini-games are booming while overseas lacks equivalent traffic aggregation, delaying platform dividends; traditional mobile exports are consolidating around top publishers with mid-tier markets contracting; numerical-value + ad-buying models are losing effectiveness; 2025 marks a "quality leap" for overseas expansion driven by tech innovation and business model upgrades; gaming success relies on genre specialization and cultural empowerment, while non-gaming success relies on industrial upgrading and model innovation.
**Metrics:** New SLG titles exceeding $10M monthly revenue; ~100 monthly in-game events for *Gossip Harbor*; >50% YoY growth for the Merge game market; Merge market size at 1/3 of Match-3; 2025 self-developed game overseas actual sales revenue: $20.455B (+10.23% YoY); mobile game contribution: $18.478B (+13.16% YoY); SLG revenue share among top 100 overseas self-developed mobile games: 49.96%; overseas revenue exceeding 100B RMB for six consecutive years.
**Evidence Types:** Industry market reports, revenue tracking data, live-ops operational metrics, comparative genre market sizing, trend analysis, industry revenue statistics, YoY growth rates, market share percentages, qualitative market trend analysis.
**Tables/Figures:** None explicitly referenced or described in the text.

**Topics:** Chinese mobile game and enterprise overseas expansion trends (2023–2026), genre-specific growth drivers (SLG, Merge/Hybrid casual), market consolidation vs. innovation, domestic vs. overseas mini-game dynamics, and PC/console (Steam) market developments.
**Entities:** Publishers/Platforms (Tencent, TikTok, Moonton, Chinese publishers, Turkish studios); Games/Titles (*Whiteout Survival*, *Kingshot*, *Last Z*, *Land of Prison*, *Gossip Harbor*, 《抓大鹅》, 《永远的蔚蓝海岸》, 《生存33天》, 《三角洲行动》, 《少女前线 2》, 《无尽对决》, 《黑神话：悟空》, 《明末：渊墟之羽》, 《饿殍：明末千里行》, 《峰火十四》); Markets/Regions (US, Japan, South Korea, Europe, China).
**Dates/Timeframes:** Late 2020 (start of product model exploration), 2023 (market testing/adjustment phase), 2024 (major title releases), 2025 (current review period, "quality leap" phase, strategic pivot), 2026 (projected industry focus), and a six-year consecutive milestone (overseas revenue >100B RMB).
**Definitions/Concepts:** SLG, Merge/Synthesis casual, Hybrid casual, LTV, secondary vs. main gameplay integration, short-drama narrative mechanics, "Adjustment" phase (contrasting 2023 product-model testing with 2025 traffic-driven retreat), "Traffic-driven打法" (user acquisition/ad-buying focus), "Numerical-value导向模型" (stat/progression-driven design paired with ad spending).
**Procedures/Strategies:** Adapting PC/Steam premium mechanics for mobile; scaling live-ops event frequency; integrating high-intensity, plot-twist narratives; benchmarking Turkish hybrid casual models; pivoting from experimental product models to established traffic-acquisition strategies; cross-regional promotion of legacy titles; platform revenue-sharing policy adjustments; leveraging gameplay fusion and cultural empowerment.
**Claims:** SLG is the largest game subcategory in 2025; Merge games achieved >50% YoY growth, now equaling one-third of the Match-3 market size; Chinese developers will successfully penetrate more genres and expand market share in 2026; the 2023–2025 adjustment period reflects a shift from product-model testing to traffic-driven methods after exploration failures; domestic mini-games are booming while overseas lacks equivalent traffic aggregation, delaying platform dividends; traditional mobile exports are consolidating around top publishers with mid-tier markets contracting; numerical-value + ad-buying models are losing effectiveness; 2025 marks a "quality leap" for overseas expansion driven by tech innovation and business model upgrades; gaming success relies on genre specialization and cultural empowerment, while non-gaming success relies on industrial upgrading and model innovation.
**Metrics:** New SLG titles exceeding $10M monthly revenue; ~100 monthly in-game events for *Gossip Harbor*; >50% YoY growth for the Merge game market; Merge market size at 1/3 of Match-3; 2025 self-developed game overseas actual sales revenue: $20.455B (+10.23% YoY); mobile game contribution: $18.478B (+13.16% YoY); SLG revenue share among top 100 overseas self-developed mobile games: 49.96%; overseas revenue exceeding 100B RMB for six consecutive years.
**Evidence Types:** Industry market reports, revenue tracking data, live-ops operational metrics, comparative genre market sizing, trend analysis, industry revenue statistics, YoY growth rates, market share percentages, qualitative market trend analysis.
**Tables/Figures:** None explicitly referenced or described in the text.
